title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Independent Server Trial
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure scoped Codex STDIO or HTTP servers, timeouts, tools, approvals, and authentication.
Configure Claude Code local, project, or user MCP scope and review shared server trust.
Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-support-runbook-11wyie: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Support Runbook into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-support-runbook-11wyie:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Support Runbook condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Support Runbook applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a connection, auth, timeout, output-limit, and version troubleshooting runbook.
